common.policyQuestion: Have a question about our policies?
common.whyBornDesc: Our guides grew up next to these parks. Local knowledge, no middlemen.
common.whyVehiclesDesc: Every safari is in a dedicated, custom-fitted 4×4 — never shared.
common.whyGroupsDesc: Maximum 6 guests per vehicle. Closer wildlife, better photos.
common.whyLicensedDesc: TATO-registered, fully bonded, and government licensed.
common.whySupportDesc: Your dedicated planner is reachable before, during, and after.
common.whyYearsDesc: Hundreds of safaris led. Every park, every season, mastered.
common.step1Desc: Tell us your travel dates, group size, budget, and dream wildlife. No rigid packages — every trip starts from scratch.
common.step2Desc: Your dedicated Arusha-based planner crafts a tailor-made route. You review, adjust, and perfect it together.
common.step3Desc: Lock in your safari with a deposit. We handle all park fees, lodge bookings, transfers, and permits.
common.step4DescTanzania: Your private guide meets you at Arusha airport. From here, Tanzania unfolds exactly as you imagined it.
common.step4DescKenya: Your private guide meets you at the airport. From here, Kenya unfolds exactly as you imagined it.
common.step4DescRwanda: Your private guide meets you at the airport. From here, Rwanda unfolds exactly as you imagined it.

home.heroTitle: Where the Wild Calls You Home
home.heroSub: Tanzania · Kenya · Rwanda — Custom safaris, born in the wilderness
home.weAreTanzania: We are Tanzania. Not an agency abroad selling Africa — a team of local experts who call the wilderness home.
home.whyBornDesc: We are not a foreign agency reselling packages. We live here, we know every trail, every guide, and every hidden camp in Tanzania.
home.whyTailorDesc: No group tours, no cookie-cutter itineraries. Every safari is built around you — your dates, your budget, and your dream.
home.whyGuidesDesc: TANAPA-certified guides with 10+ years in the field. Born in the wilderness, fluent in wildlife and culture.
home.whyCareDesc: From airport pickup to final drop-off, we handle every detail. 24/7 support throughout your entire journey.
home.realStories: Real stories from real guests — unedited, unsponsored, unforgettable.
home.joinTravelers: Join 200+ travelers who've explored Africa with us — and came back for more.
home.whenToSafari: When to Safari in East Africa
home.timingDesc: Every month offers something special — here's your guide to timing your visit perfectly.
home.migrationTrackerSub: Approximate herd activity & location by month
home.kiliTrackerSub: Summit success likelihood & trail conditions by month
home.migrationBestWindows: Best windows: Jul–Oct for river crossings & peak drama. Jan–Feb for calving season in Ngorongoro. Avoid Apr–May (long rains).
home.kiliBestWindows: Best windows: Jan–Feb & Jul–Sep. Avoid Apr–May (long rains) — muddy trails reduce summit success to ~60%.
home.ctaDesc: Tell us your dream. We'll design a tailor-made safari around you — your dates, your budget, your bucket list.
home.responseTimeNote: Average response time: under 2 hours · No commitment required
home.newsletterDesc: Safari tips, wildlife spotting guides, monthly travel inspiration and exclusive early-bird deals — delivered straight to your inbox.
home.exploreByRegionDesc: Select a country to view national parks, wildlife reserves and key destinations across East Africa.
home.tanzaniaDesc: Home to the Serengeti, Kilimanjaro, Ngorongoro Crater and Zanzibar — Africa's most complete safari destination.
home.kenyaDesc: Birthplace of the Great Migration and home to the iconic Masai Mara. World-class big cat country.
home.rwandaDesc: "Land of a Thousand Hills" — famous for mountain gorilla trekking, pristine rainforests and warm hospitality.
home.dc5Wildlife: White Sand Beaches · Coral Reefs · Swahili Culture
home.pkg2H2: Dhow sunset cruise in Stone Town
home.pkg3H1: No long drives — maximum game time
home.pkg3H2: Ultra-exclusive camps deep in the Serengeti
home.blog0Title: The Complete Guide to the Great Wildebeest Migration
home.blog0Excerpt: Every year, 1.5 million wildebeest cross the Serengeti in one of nature's greatest spectacles. Here's everything you need to know to witness it.
home.blog1Title: How to Climb Kilimanjaro: Routes, Cost & Everything You Need to Know
home.blog1Excerpt: Africa's highest peak doesn't require technical climbing skills — but it demands preparation. Here's the complete 2025 guide to every route and what to expect.
home.blog2Title: Zanzibar Travel Guide: Beaches, Stone Town & Safari Combos
home.blog2Excerpt: White coral sand, turquoise Indian Ocean water, ancient Swahili culture — Zanzibar is the perfect final chapter to any Tanzania safari.
home.rev0Text: I've done safaris in Kenya and South Africa, but Tanzania with The Extreme Wilderness was on another level. No crowds, no cookie-cutter experiences — pure, authentic wilderness. The balloon over the Serengeti at sunrise was life-changing.
home.rev1Text: Wow, just wow, what an experience! My girlfriend and I had a 5-day tour of Lake Manyara, Serengeti and Ngorongoro Crater and it was nothing short of spectacular. Our guide George's enthusiasm for Tanzania and the wildlife was so contagious. We saw so many animals and managed to spot the Big 5!! He definitely went above and beyond.
home.rev2Text: Phenomenal! Upon our arrival Mike called us up as soon as we landed. The trip he put together for us was more than we could have expected. From our very first game drive we were blown away by the wildlife we encountered. Mike showed incredible passion, professionalism, and people skills. We would go back in a heartbeat.
home.rev3Text: Unforgettable! We completed a 5-day tour that was genuinely life-changing. The accommodations, transportation, and game drives were all top-notch. Our guide was extraordinary — deeply passionate about wildlife and conservation, and his knowledge made every drive educational and thrilling. Extreme Wilderness truly delivered beyond our expectations.
home.rev4Text: Professional and easy going — that perfectly sums up Extreme Wilderness. Our guide Mike was very friendly, easy to talk to, and deeply knowledgeable about the wildlife and ecosystems. He made every game drive exciting and informative. The whole team was responsive, efficient, and responsible throughout the entire trip. Cannot recommend them enough.
home.rev5Text: It was my first time visiting Tanzania and experiencing the magical wilderness — I was so excited. A friend recommended Extreme Wilderness and I cannot thank him enough. They were amazingly quick to answer my inquiry and helped us get the best accommodations. When we touched down in Arusha, they welcomed us like royals — a bottle of champagne and exotic Tanzanian fruits. We were all so pleasantly surprised!
home.rev6Text: Great experience! We had an amazing time during this safari journey. Mike was always present and left a great impression — he was warm, professional, and made sure we saw as much wildlife as possible. It was one of the most extraordinary experiences we have ever had in Tanzania. I would not hesitate to book with Extreme Wilderness again.
home.rev7Text: The Extreme Wilderness gave us the most incredible experience of our lives. Our guide Samuel knew exactly where to find the leopard we'd been hoping to see — and delivered it on our last morning. Every detail was perfect. We'll be back for Kilimanjaro!
home.rev8Text: A dream safari with Extreme Wilderness Adventure — and the perfect choice for families! Everything was so well organised, from the airport pick-up to the game drives. Our guide was incredibly knowledgeable and patient with the kids. I would highly recommend Extreme Wilderness to anyone looking for an unforgettable African safari experience.
home.rev9Text: Well organised and fun! Before leaving we were uncertain about what to expect, but our guide Peter was very efficient and always showed up on time. He was friendly, knowledgeable, and knew exactly where to take us to maximise our game-viewing. The routes were perfect and the tent camp was a lovely bonus surprise. We saw all the Big Five and had an absolute blast!
home.rev10Text: Notre lune de miel parfaite — safari au Serengeti puis Zanzibar. The team arranged everything flawlessly and our French-speaking guide was exceptional. We saw all the Big Five in three days. Magnifique!
home.rev11Text: So helpful! The team at Extreme Wilderness was incredibly supportive in planning our trip and answered all our questions promptly. Our guide was knowledgeable and enthusiastic throughout. It was a wonderful experience in Tanzania that I will cherish for years to come.

trekking.heroDesc: Africa's highest peak. Six distinct routes. One unforgettable summit.
trekking.mapNote: Custom topographic map · {name} · TANAPA approved trail
trekking.pricingDesc: All prices are per person and include everything listed below. Reduced rates for groups of 4+.
trekking.readyDesc: Tell us your dates, fitness level and budget — we'll handle everything else. Average response: under 2 hours.
trekking.heroTagline: Africa's highest peak. The world's tallest free-standing mountain. Guided by Tanzania-born experts who have stood on the summit hundreds of times.
trekking.packSubtitle: 12 essentials your porters won't carry for you — everything else is handled.
trekking.gear1item1Desc: Thermal base layers, fleece mid-layer, waterproof shell jacket & trousers
trekking.gear1item2Desc: Rated to -10°C / 14°F. Warmth is critical above 4,000m at night
trekking.gear1item3Desc: Waterproof, ankle-supporting, well broken-in before the climb
trekking.gear1item4Desc: Keep debris and water out of boots on volcanic scree sections
trekking.gear2item1Desc: Collapsible poles reduce knee strain significantly on descent
trekking.gear2item2Desc: Essential for summit night (3am start). Bring spare batteries
trekking.gear2item3Desc: Keep electronics and sleeping bag dry — rain is unpredictable
trekking.gear3item1Desc: Diamox (acetazolamide) — consult your doctor before the climb
trekking.gear3item2Desc: Blister treatment, ibuprofen, anti-nausea tablets, bandages
trekking.gear3item3Desc: SPF 50+ sunscreen, UV-blocking sunglasses, wide-brim hat
trekking.gear4item1Desc: 2–3 litre water bladder or bottles. Water freezes at summit
trekking.gear4item2Desc: High-calorie snacks: nuts, chocolate, energy gels for summit push
trekking.proTipText: The single biggest mistake climbers make is packing too heavy. Every extra kilogram will slow your summit attempt. Our porters carry your main bag (max 15 kg) — keep your day pack to 5–7 kg with just the essentials.
trekking.whenToSummitSubtitle: Kilimanjaro never closes — but the mountain rewards those who choose their window wisely.
trekking.season1Desc: Cold, dry and clear. Every route fully open. Your best chance of standing on Uhuru Peak.
trekking.season1b1: Clear summit skies on most days
trekking.season1b3: Dry underfoot — trails at their best
trekking.season2Desc: Warm and generally dry. Wildlife-rich around the mountain. Fewer crowds, still great summit odds.
trekking.season3Desc: Long and short rains bring muddy trails, reduced summit visibility, and lower success rates.
trekking.season3Warning: We still run climbs on request — conditions vary significantly year to year.
trekking.beforeClimbSubtitle: Field-tested knowledge from our guides, written for climbers who want to be genuinely prepared.
trekking.article1Title: Machame vs Lemosho: Which Route Is Actually Better?
trekking.article1Desc: Our guides have led thousands on both routes. Here's an honest side-by-side — terrain, success rates, crowd levels and which suits your fitness level.
trekking.article2Title: How to Train for Kilimanjaro: A 12-Week Fitness Plan
trekking.article2Desc: You don't need to be an athlete to summit Kilimanjaro. But preparation is everything. Here's exactly what to do in the 12 weeks before your climb.
trekking.article3Title: Altitude Sickness on Kilimanjaro: Prevention & Treatment
trekking.article3Desc: AMS affects up to 75% of climbers. Understanding the symptoms, prevention steps, and when to descend could be the difference between summit and safety.
trekking.article4Title: What Does a Kilimanjaro Climb Actually Cost in 2026?
trekking.article4Desc: The real numbers — park fees, tips, gear, flights. We break down every cost so you can budget accurately and avoid hidden surprises.
trekking.ctaSubtitle: Tell us your fitness level, dates, and budget — we'll match you with the perfect Kilimanjaro route.
trekking.routeMapSubtitle: Select a route to explore its map, difficulty, and what makes it unique.
trekking.included4: Full board meals on the mountain

contact.heroDesc: Tell us your dream. Our team responds within 2 hours with a personalised itinerary — no obligation, just honest advice from people who live this every day.
contact.whatsappDesc: Chat directly with our safari team for an instant response.

about.heroDesc: We are Tanzanian guides who grew tired of watching foreign operators sell Africa from abroad. So in 2022, we built something different — a safari company that is authentically, entirely of this land.
about.localDesc1: When you book with a locally owned operator, your money stays in Africa. It pays fair wages to guides, porters, and camp staff. It funds the community schools and water projects that our team personally support. It does not disappear into a head office in London or New York.
about.pullQuote: Our knowledge cannot be bought from a textbook. It comes from being born here, growing up in the bush, and spending decades learning what no guidebook will ever tell you.
about.localDesc2: Safari quality comes from lived experience, not glossy brochures. We know which crossing on the Mara River is most active in August. We know the ranger who has been tracking a particular pride for six years. We know the back roads and the secret viewpoints that group-tour operators never visit.
about.localDesc3: That is the difference. And it is a difference you will feel every single day in the bush.
about.locallyOwnedBody: Born and based in Arusha, not a foreign franchise. Every dollar spent stays in Tanzania and supports local conservation.
about.expertGuidesBody: TANAPA-certified, multi-lingual guides who grew up tracking wildlife in the parks they now share with you.
about.privateOnlyBody: No group tours, no strangers in your vehicle. Every safari is exclusive to you and the people you choose to bring.
about.yearsBody: Running safaris since 2022 — over 200 travellers, thousands of sunrises in the bush, and counting.
about.teamDesc: Born in Tanzania. Passionate about wildlife. Ready to share it with you.
about.ctaDesc: Get in touch — our team will design your perfect East Africa adventure and have a personalised itinerary in your inbox within 24 hours. No generic packages, no pushy sales. Just honest advice from people who live this every day.
about.responseNote: We typically respond within 2–4 hours during business hours (EAT, UTC+3).

blog.title: Safari Stories & Travel Guides
blog.heroSubtitle: Expert guides, wildlife insights, and travel inspiration from our team on the ground in Tanzania.
blog.noPostsInCategory: No posts in this category yet.
blog.customItinerariesText: Our expert team creates custom itineraries for every budget and timeframe. Get your personalised quote within 24 hours.

blog.arushaResponds: Our Arusha-based team responds within 24 hours — no sales pressure.

destinations.heroSubtitle: From the Serengeti's endless plains to the southern wilderness of Ruaha and the chimpanzee forests of the west — Tanzania offers three distinct safari circuits, each extraordinary.
destinations.box2Heading: Why Book With The Extreme Wilderness
destinations.extrasSubtitle: Every great Tanzania safari begins in Arusha and often ends on the white sands of Zanzibar.
destinations.seasonHeading: Best Time to Safari in Tanzania
destinations.seasonSubtitle: Tanzania is a year-round destination. Each season brings different advantages — from peak migration crossings to lush green landscapes and calving season drama.
destinations.migrationHeading: The Great Wildebeest Migration — Follow It Year-Round
destinations.circuitNorthernTagline: The Classic Safari — Most Wildlife, Most Icons
destinations.season1Tip: Peak safari season. Best wildlife across all parks. Migration in Serengeti/Mara (Jul–Oct). Book 6–12 months ahead.
destinations.season2Tip: Short afternoon showers. Lush landscapes return. Good bird watching. Less crowded with better rates.
destinations.season3Tip: Excellent game viewing. Wildebeest calving (Jan–Feb) in southern Serengeti. Zanzibar at its best.
destinations.season4Tip: Heaviest rains. Some roads impassable. Very low visitor numbers and best lodge rates. Lush green scenery.
destinations.introBody: Tanzania protects more land under conservation than almost any other country on Earth — 28% of its total land area. This extraordinary commitment produces an unrivalled density of wildlife across three distinct safari circuits, each offering a completely different experience. The Northern Circuit delivers the iconic Serengeti and Ngorongoro classics; the Southern Circuit rewards those seeking true wilderness solitude; and the Western Circuit opens the door to chimpanzees, hippo spectacles, and Africa's largest lake. At The Extreme Wilderness — born and based in Arusha — these are our home parks.
destinations.migrationDesc: The Migration never stops — it just moves. Calving in the Serengeti's south (Jan–Feb), north through the Seronera (Mar–Jun), Grumeti River crossings in the west (Jun–Jul), dramatic Mara River crossings in the north (Jul–Oct), then back south again. We design itineraries around wherever the herds are.
destinations.ctaHeading: Let's Plan Your Perfect Tanzania Safari

kenya.heroSubtitle: From the thundering hooves of the Great Migration to the gentle giants of Amboseli — Kenya offers Africa's most iconic wildlife spectacles, ancient cultures, and pristine Indian Ocean beaches.
kenya.box1Heading: Kenya's Wild Places at a Glance
kenya.box2Heading: Why Book With The Extreme Wilderness
kenya.destSectionSubtitle: Each destination offers something no other place on Earth can match. Combined with Tanzania, they form the ultimate East Africa safari circuit.
kenya.seasonHeading: Best Time to Safari in Kenya
kenya.seasonSubtitle: Kenya is a year-round destination, but timing your visit around the Great Migration crossing or the dry season maximises your wildlife encounters.
kenya.migrationCalloutHeading: The Great Migration — Nature's Greatest Show
kenya.ctaHeading: Let's Plan Your Kenya Safari Together

rwanda.heroSubtitle: Meet the mountain gorillas. Trek ancient rainforests with chimpanzees. Safari the savannahs. Rwanda is small in size but extraordinary in depth — one of Africa's most intimate and transformative destinations.
rwanda.box1Heading: Rwanda's Wild Places at a Glance
rwanda.box2Heading: Why Book With The Extreme Wilderness
rwanda.permitHeading: Mountain Gorilla Permits — Book Well in Advance
rwanda.destSectionSubtitle: From volcanic gorilla forests to savannah plains, ancient rainforests to lakeside towns — Rwanda packs remarkable diversity into a country smaller than Belgium.
rwanda.seasonSubtitle: Rwanda is a year-round destination. Gorilla trekking is possible every day, but dry seasons bring easier trails and more comfortable conditions.
rwanda.ctaHeading: Let's Plan Your Rwanda Adventure Together
rwanda.gorillaPermitNotice: Only 96 gorilla permits issued per day — book well in advance

experiences.heroSubtitle: Twelve ways to encounter East Africa — each one crafted by guides who were born here, live here, and love it deeply.
experiences.introParagraph1: For fifteen years, The Extreme Wilderness has been crafting safari journeys for people who want more than a tour — they want a transformation.
experiences.introParagraph2: East Africa is enormous and extraordinarily varied. We're here to help you find your experience — the one that fits your pace, your wonder, your family.
experiences.step1Desc: Fill in our short form — dates, budget, interests, who you're travelling with. No commitment.
experiences.step2Desc: A dedicated safari expert designs a tailor-made itinerary within 24 hours.
experiences.step3Desc: Adjust every detail — we iterate until it's exactly right. No rush, no pressure.
experiences.step4Desc: We handle every detail from airport to camp to beach. You just show up and be present.
experiences.ctaHeading: Start planning your East Africa journey
experiences.ctaSubtitle: Every itinerary begins with a conversation. Tell us where you want to go — or let us surprise you. No obligation, just possibilities.
experiences.newsletterText: Migration updates, seasonal guides, new camp openings and field notes from our guides — delivered monthly. No noise, only the good stuff.
experiences.introHeading: East Africa is not one experience.
experiences.introHeadingSecondary: It is a hundred, and we know them all.
experiences.introParagraph1Full: For fifteen years, The Extreme Wilderness has been crafting safari journeys for people who want more than a tour — they want a transformation. We are a Tanzanian-owned, Arusha-based operator with deep roots in the wilderness and personal relationships with the camps, lodges and communities we work with.
experiences.introParagraph2Full: East Africa is enormous and extraordinarily varied. A week in the Serengeti is nothing like a week in Ruaha. Gorilla trekking in Rwanda is nothing like chimpanzee trekking in Mahale. We're here to help you find your experience — the one that fits your pace, your wonder, your family.
experiences.quoteText: We didn't just see Africa. We felt it. The guides at The Extreme Wilderness gave us something we couldn't have found anywhere else — a genuine, unhurried, deeply personal experience of the wild.

itineraries.heroSubtitle: Every route we design moves with the rhythms of the wild — not against them. Hand-picked camps, expert resident guides, and a level of detail most operators never reach.
itineraries.signatureEditionsSubtitle: Four expertly designed journeys built for travellers who don't want ordinary. Each one is a starting point — we shape it entirely around you.
itineraries.completeCollectionSubtitle: From 5-day fly-in circuits to 12-day cross-border adventures — each package is a blueprint, not a box.
itineraries.processSubtitle: We don't sell packages. We build itineraries. Here's exactly how it works.
itineraries.step1Desc: Tell us when you want to travel, how many days you have, and what you most want to experience. No question is too vague.
itineraries.step2Desc: Our Arusha-based team builds a custom route around your dates, budget and interests — camps and daily rhythms mapped from the ground up.
itineraries.step3Desc: We adjust, swap and rework until every detail is exactly right. Most clients take 2–3 iterations. We never rush.
itineraries.step4Desc: Your expert guides take it from here. Transfers, camps, park fees and all meals — fully handled. You just show up ready.
itineraries.whenToGoText: Tanzania's dry seasons offer the best game viewing. The Great Migration river crossings peak July–September. Calving season (Ndutu) runs January–March. December and February are excellent shoulder months with fewer crowds.
itineraries.included1: All game drives in custom 4×4 vehicles
itineraries.included5: All meals at camp or lodge
itineraries.faq1a: Every itinerary is a starting point. We shape 100% of the journey around your travel window, interests and group size.
itineraries.faq2q: What is the minimum group size?
itineraries.faq2a: We operate from 1 person upward. Solo and couple safaris travel in private vehicles at no extra charge.
itineraries.faq3q: How far in advance should I book?
itineraries.faq3a: Peak season (Jun–Oct): 6–12 months. Shoulder: 3–6 months is comfortable. Last-minute is sometimes possible.
itineraries.gettingHereTip1: Fly into Kilimanjaro International Airport (JRO) — 1 hr from Arusha
itineraries.gettingHereTip2: Direct connections via Nairobi, Addis Ababa, Doha, Dubai or Amsterdam
itineraries.gettingHereTip3: We arrange all airport transfers from JRO to your first camp or lodge
itineraries.gettingHereTip4: Tanzania e-Visa available online — allow 3–10 business days to process
itineraries.gettingHereTip5: Yellow fever certificate required if arriving from endemic countries
itineraries.ctaSubtitle: Share your travel dates and what moves you. We'll build something extraordinary — entirely around you, no templates, no shortcuts.
itineraries.noCommitment: No commitment. We respond within 24 hours.
itineraries.newsletterText: Migration updates, new itineraries, exclusive offers and field notes from our guides — delivered monthly.

privacy.heroSubtitle: We are committed to protecting your personal data. This policy explains exactly what we collect, why, and how you stay in control — including your rights under GDPR.
privacy.s1p1: The Extreme Wilderness ("we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your personal information. This Privacy Policy explains what data we collect when you visit our website or enquire about our safari services, how we use it, and your rights under applicable data protection laws — including the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the UK GDPR.
privacy.s1tip: By using our website or submitting an enquiry, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this policy.
privacy.s2p1: The Extreme Wilderness is a Tanzania-registered safari operator based in Arusha, Tanzania. We design and operate custom safaris, Kilimanjaro climbs, and wildlife experiences across Tanzania, Kenya, and Rwanda.
privacy.s3item1: Full name, email address, phone number
privacy.s3item3: Travel dates, group size, dietary requirements, and relevant medical conditions
privacy.s3item4: Payment information (processed by our payment provider — we do not store card details)
privacy.s3item5: Correspondence via email, contact forms, or chat
privacy.s3item6: IP address, browser type, and device information
privacy.s3item7: Pages visited, time on site, and referring URLs
privacy.s4p1: We use your personal information to:
privacy.s4item1: Respond to safari enquiries and prepare personalised itineraries
privacy.s4item2: Process bookings and issue travel documents
privacy.s4item3: Communicate pre-trip details, updates, and post-trip follow-ups
privacy.s4item4: Send promotional emails and newsletters (only where you have opted in)
privacy.s4item5: Comply with legal obligations (e.g. Tanzania Tourism Authority reporting)
privacy.s4item6: Improve our website and services through anonymised analytics
privacy.s4callout: Legal basis (GDPR): Contract performance for booking processing · Legitimate interests for customer service and business improvement · Consent for marketing communications.
privacy.s5p1: We do not sell your personal data. We share it only with trusted third parties necessary to deliver your safari:
privacy.s5item1: Partner lodges, camps, and hotels — to confirm your accommodation
privacy.s5item2: Local ground operators and guides — to facilitate activities
privacy.s5item3: Airlines and transfer services — where booked through us
privacy.s5item4: Payment processors — to handle transactions securely
privacy.s5item5: Analytics providers (e.g. Google Analytics) — using anonymised data only
privacy.s5item6: Regulatory authorities — where required by Tanzanian or other applicable law
privacy.s5tip: All third parties are contractually required to protect your data and use it only for the stated purpose.
privacy.s6p1: As a Tanzania-based company serving global clients, your data may be transferred to and processed in countries outside the European Economic Area (EEA). Where this occurs, we take steps to ensure appropriate safeguards are in place, such as standard contractual clauses approved by the European Commission.
privacy.s7p1: We retain your personal data for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes described in this policy, or as required by law.
privacy.s7card2Sub: Opt out any time, instantly honoured
privacy.s8p1: Depending on your location, you may have the following rights regarding your personal data:
privacy.s8r1Desc: Request a copy of the data we hold about you
privacy.s8r2Desc: Ask us to correct inaccurate or incomplete data
privacy.s8r3Desc: Request deletion of your data ("right to be forgotten")
privacy.s8r4Desc: Ask us to limit how we process your data
privacy.s8r5Desc: Receive your data in a machine-readable format
privacy.s8r6Desc: Object to processing based on legitimate interests
privacy.s8r7Desc: For marketing, at any time with immediate effect
privacy.s8callout: To exercise any right, email us at {email}. We respond within 30 days. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your national data protection authority.
privacy.s9p1: Our website uses cookies to improve your experience and analyse site traffic:
privacy.s9c1Desc: Necessary for the website to function correctly. Cannot be disabled.
privacy.s9c2Desc: Help us understand how visitors interact with the site (Google Analytics, anonymised).
privacy.s9c3Desc: Remember your settings and choices for a better experience.
privacy.s9note: You can control or disable cookies through your browser settings. Disabling certain cookies may affect website functionality.
privacy.s10p1: Our website is not directed at children under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children without verified parental consent. If you believe we have inadvertently collected data from a child, please contact us and we will delete it promptly.
privacy.s11p1: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction.
privacy.s11tip: Our website uses HTTPS encryption for all data transmissions. Payment data is handled by PCI-DSS compliant processors — we never see or store card numbers.
privacy.s12p1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. The effective date at the top of this page will reflect the most recent revision. We encourage you to review this page periodically. Continued use of our website after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.
privacy.s13p1: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your data, please contact us — we aim to respond within 2 working days.

terms.heroSubtitle: Please read before booking. These terms govern all safaris, trekking expeditions, and travel experiences arranged by The Extreme Wilderness.
terms.noticePillPrefix: By making a booking you confirm you have read and agree to these Terms in full. Questions? Email
terms.s1p1: These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") govern all bookings and services provided by The Extreme Wilderness ("we", "us", "our"), a safari operator registered in Tanzania. By making a booking or using our website, you ("the client") confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms in full.
terms.s1tip: These Terms constitute the entire agreement between you and us and supersede all prior agreements, representations, and understandings.
terms.s2p1: A booking is confirmed once we have received your signed booking form (or written confirmation via email) and the required deposit. We will issue a written confirmation within 48 hours.
terms.s2callout: For bookings made within 60 days of departure, full payment is required at the time of booking. We reserve the right to decline any booking at our sole discretion.
terms.s3p1: All prices are in USD unless stated otherwise, quoted per person based on your group size and itinerary.
terms.s3i2: All game drives and listed activities
terms.s3i3: Park entrance fees and conservation levies
terms.s3i4: All meals as specified (FB / HB / BB)
terms.s3tip: We reserve the right to adjust prices due to significant changes in park fees, fuel costs, or government levies. Confirmed bookings are only affected if such changes occur more than 30 days before departure.
terms.s4p1: All cancellations must be received in writing (email is acceptable). Fees are calculated from the date we receive your written notice:
terms.s4callout: We strongly recommend purchasing comprehensive travel insurance that includes cancellation cover. These fees may be fully recoverable through your insurer.
terms.s5p1: We will only cancel a confirmed booking in exceptional circumstances beyond our control (see Section 11: Force Majeure). In the unlikely event we must cancel, we will offer you:
terms.s5item1: A full refund of all monies paid to us, or
terms.s5item2: An alternative trip of equivalent value, if available
terms.s5callout: We are not liable for ancillary costs (flights, visas, vaccinations, etc.) incurred by you in the event of cancellation. This is one of the key reasons travel insurance is mandatory.
terms.s6p1: We plan itineraries carefully, but East African safari conditions can change rapidly. We reserve the right to alter itineraries, accommodation, or routes where necessary to ensure safety, or due to circumstances beyond our control — including road conditions, wildlife movement, weather, or park authority decisions.
terms.s6tip: We will always endeavour to provide alternatives of equal or greater value. No refunds will be given for minor itinerary changes that do not materially affect the overall quality of your trip.
terms.s7p1: By booking with us, you confirm that:
terms.s7item1: You are physically fit and medically able to undertake the activities booked
terms.s7item2: You have disclosed any medical conditions, dietary requirements, or disabilities that may affect your experience
terms.s7item3: You hold a valid passport with at least 6 months validity beyond your return date, and all required visas
terms.s7item4: You will follow the instructions of our guides and local park authority rangers at all times
terms.s7item5: You accept that wildlife encounters are inherently unpredictable — we cannot guarantee specific sightings
terms.s7item6: You will not harass or disturb wildlife, and will adhere to all park rules and regulations
terms.s7item7: You take sole responsibility for any personal property lost, stolen, or damaged during the trip
terms.s8callout: Travel insurance is mandatory for all clients. You must provide proof of cover before or at the start of your trip. We reserve the right to decline participation to any client without adequate insurance.
terms.s8calloutBold: Travel insurance is mandatory for all clients.
terms.s8p1: Your policy must include, at minimum:
terms.s8ins1Desc: Covers fees if you cancel before departure
terms.s8ins4Desc: Trekking above 4,000m if applicable to your booking
terms.s9p1: The Extreme Wilderness acts as a tour operator arranging services on your behalf. While we take all reasonable precautions for your safety and wellbeing, we cannot accept liability for:
terms.s9item1: Personal injury, illness, death, or loss caused by events beyond our reasonable control
terms.s9item2: Actions or omissions of third-party service providers (airlines, hotels, etc.)
terms.s9item3: Theft, damage, or loss of personal belongings
terms.s9item4: Natural disasters, civil unrest, government actions, or other force majeure events
terms.s9item5: Failure to obtain required travel documents (visas, vaccinations, etc.)
terms.s9item6: Loss of enjoyment or indirect consequential losses
terms.s9callout: Where liability cannot be excluded by law, our total liability shall not exceed the total amount paid for the trip. Nothing in these Terms limits liability for death or personal injury caused by our proven negligence.
terms.s10p1: You are responsible for ensuring you are in adequate health to participate in your chosen activities. We recommend consulting your doctor or a travel health clinic before departure for vaccination and malaria prophylaxis advice.
terms.s10p2: Tanzania, Kenya, and Rwanda may require proof of yellow fever vaccination for entry from certain countries. Please verify current requirements with your doctor and the relevant embassies before travel.
terms.s10tip: If you have pre-existing medical conditions, disabilities, or special requirements, please inform us at the time of booking. We will do our utmost to accommodate your needs, but cannot guarantee this in all cases.
terms.s11p1: Neither party shall be liable for failure to perform obligations under these Terms if such failure results from circumstances beyond reasonable control, including but not limited to:
terms.s11item1: Natural disasters and extreme weather events
terms.s11item3: War, civil unrest, or acts of terrorism
terms.s11item5: Strikes or industrial action affecting travel infrastructure
terms.s11tip: In a force majeure situation, we will use reasonable endeavours to reschedule your trip or offer an alternative. If rescheduling is not possible, refunds will be subject to any non-recoverable costs already incurred on your behalf.
terms.s12p1: If you have a complaint during your trip, please raise it immediately with your guide or our local representative. Complaints not raised during the trip are significantly harder to address after the fact.
terms.s12p2Prefix: If your complaint remains unresolved, submit it in writing to
terms.s12p2Suffix: within 28 days of your return. We will acknowledge within 5 working days and aim to resolve within 28 days.
terms.s13p1: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the United Republic of Tanzania. Any disputes arising from these Terms shall first be subject to good-faith negotiation.
terms.s13callout: If unresolved through negotiation, disputes shall be referred to arbitration in Arusha, Tanzania.
terms.s14p1: Your personal data is handled in accordance with our Privacy Policy, which is incorporated into these Terms by reference.
terms.s15p1: For any questions about these Terms before or after making a booking, please contact us:

travelInfo.heroSubtitle: Everything you need to know before your Tanzania safari or Kilimanjaro trek — from the best time to visit and health requirements to packing essentials and currency.
travelInfo.qf5Value: Required for most nationalities — available on arrival or e-visa online ($50 USD)
travelInfo.qf6Value: Expected: $10–20/day for guides; $5–10/day for camp staff
travelInfo.s1b1: Tanzania is extraordinary in every month of the year — there is always somewhere remarkable to go and something unmissable to see. That said, the season you visit shapes your experience significantly, so choosing the right time for your priorities matters.
travelInfo.s1b2: The dry season from June to October is widely considered the best time for wildlife viewing. The vegetation thins, animals concentrate around permanent water sources, and roads are at their most accessible. The Great Migration river crossings in the northern Serengeti peak in July and August. Expect clear skies, comfortable daytime temperatures, and cooler evenings. Book well in advance — this is the most in-demand period.
travelInfo.s1h3: November to May — Wet Season (Green Season)
travelInfo.s1b3: The short rains (November) and long rains (March–May) bring lush, dramatically green landscapes and significantly fewer tourists. December to March is particularly rewarding: the calving season in the Ndutu area of the southern Serengeti delivers extraordinary predator-prey drama as 8,000 wildebeest calves are born every day. Accommodation prices are lower, parks are quieter, and birdlife is at its most spectacular. Some camps close in April and May, and roads in the southern parks can become impassable.
travelInfo.s1tr1c3: Calving season, Ndutu; fewer tourists; excellent predator action
travelInfo.s1tr2c3: Lush landscape; green season discounts; some camps closed
travelInfo.s1tr3c3: Peak wildlife; Mara River crossings (Jul–Oct); best visibility
travelInfo.s1tr4c3: Good value; excellent birding; landscape greening
travelInfo.s1tr5c3: Quiet parks; herds moving south; pleasant weather
travelInfo.s2b1: Under International Health Regulations, travellers arriving from yellow fever endemic countries must present a valid yellow fever vaccination certificate. We advise all clients to consult a travel health clinic or GP at least six to eight weeks before departure. Recommended vaccinations for Tanzania typically include hepatitis A, typhoid, and tetanus. Proof of COVID-19 status requirements should be checked against current government guidance close to your departure date.
travelInfo.s2b2: Tanzania is a malaria zone. Consult your doctor about an appropriate antimalarial medication before you travel — options include Malarone, Doxycycline, and Lariam, each with different profiles and suitability. In addition to medication, bring a DEET-based insect repellent and pack long-sleeved clothing for evenings. Mosquito nets are provided at all lodges and camps we use.
travelInfo.s2b3: Comprehensive travel health insurance is mandatory before travelling to Tanzania. We strongly recommend taking out a temporary AMREF Flying Doctor Service membership, which covers emergency medical evacuation from remote areas across East Africa — an important safeguard when you are in the wilderness, hours from the nearest hospital.
travelInfo.s3b1: Tanzania's official currency is the Tanzanian Shilling (TZS). US Dollars are widely accepted for safari expenses, tips, and major purchases. We recommend bringing a mix of USD cash (in clean, undamaged bills printed after 2009) and a Visa or Mastercard for city use. ATMs are available in Arusha, Dar es Salaam, and Zanzibar.
travelInfo.s3b2: Currency exchange is available at Kilimanjaro and Julius Nyerere international airports, all major commercial banks, and licensed bureau de change offices in Arusha and other towns. Avoid exchanging money with unofficial street dealers.
travelInfo.s3b3: Personal items such as cameras, binoculars, and laptops are imported duty-free. Professional or commercial filming equipment, musical instruments, and recording devices may require a customs bond guaranteeing re-export — check with the Tanzania Revenue Authority before travelling if you are bringing extensive gear. Firearms and hunting weapons require a special import permit obtained in advance. Retain receipts for all souvenir and handicraft purchases, and ensure any wildlife trophies are accompanied by proper CITES documentation for presentation at customs on departure.
travelInfo.s4b1: Safari vehicles, light aircraft, and bush camps all impose practical luggage limits. Soft-sided bags are strongly preferred over rigid suitcases — they compress into luggage compartments more easily. For fly-in safaris, most light aircraft operators enforce a 15 kg soft-bag limit per person. Pack efficiently and leave the wheeled suitcase at your Arusha or Nairobi hotel for days spent in the bush.
travelInfo.s4b2: Neutral, muted colours — khaki, olive, beige, tan, brown — are the safari standard. Bright colours and white attract insects and can startle wildlife; patterns are harder for animals to read than solid neutrals. Natural fibres such as cotton and linen breathe well in the heat. A light fleece or down jacket is essential for cold mornings and evenings, particularly in the Ngorongoro highlands and at altitude on Kilimanjaro. Long sleeves and trousers at dusk reduce mosquito exposure.
travelInfo.s4list1: Light, neutral-coloured shirts and trousers (cotton or linen)
travelInfo.s4list2: Fleece or lightweight down jacket for mornings and evenings
travelInfo.s4list3: Comfortable, broken-in walking shoes or light trail runners
travelInfo.s4list7: Swimsuit — for lodges, beach extensions, and boat safaris
travelInfo.s4list10: Power bank — electricity in remote camps can be intermittent
travelInfo.s5b1: Tanzania is one of the most stable and safe countries in sub-Saharan Africa, with a long record of peaceful tourism. The national parks are accessed in dedicated safari vehicles with certified guides — the risk profile inside the parks is extremely low. As in any country, exercise normal awareness in urban areas, avoid displaying expensive equipment in crowded places, and keep copies of your passport and travel documents.
travelInfo.s5b2: Follow your guide's instructions at all times when in or near wildlife areas. Do not exit the vehicle except at designated areas or with a certified armed ranger escort. Never approach wildlife on foot without a guide. At night in camps and lodges, do not walk between tents alone if wildlife may be present — camp staff will escort you.
travelInfo.ctaHeading: Not sure which month is right for you?
travelInfo.ctaSubtitle: The best time to visit Tanzania depends entirely on what you want to see and do. Tell us your priorities — Mara River crossings, calving season, Kilimanjaro weather windows, Zanzibar conditions — and we will recommend the ideal travel window for your specific trip.
travelInfo.contactSubtitle: Our team in Arusha is available seven days a week to answer pre-travel questions about health requirements, visa applications, what to pack, and anything else you need to know before your trip.
